source.title: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease and dyspepsia in adults: investigation and management
source.population: Adults aged 18 years and over with dyspepsia, symptoms suggestive of gastro-oesophageal reflux disease, or both.
source.setting: Community, primary-care, investigation, review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult reflux and dyspepsia symptom, alarm, investigation, review, and clinician-discussed management fields; the legacy workflow has no adult-only filter and UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Scope and recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.2 and 1.4.1 — symptom profile and common care

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented reflux management, medication-plan discussion, review, and specialist referral where symptoms do not respond or remain unexplained; no dose or autonomous treatment text is mapped.

exact_location.heading: Scope and recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.2 and 1.4.1 — symptom profile and common care

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

items.rationale: The exact adult dyspepsia section supports interval epigastric pain, heartburn, regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, meal context, precipitants, and change without confirming dyspepsia cause or response.

items.final_wording: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

source.title: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease and dyspepsia in adults: investigation and management
source.population: Adults aged 18 years and over with dyspepsia, symptoms suggestive of gastro-oesophageal reflux disease, or both.
source.setting: Community, primary-care, investigation, review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult reflux and dyspepsia symptom, alarm, investigation, review, and clinician-discussed management fields; the legacy workflow has no adult-only filter and UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Scope and recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.2 and 1.4.1 — symptom profile and common care

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

added_items.rationale: The exact adult dyspepsia section supports interval epigastric pain, heartburn, regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, meal context, precipitants, and change without confirming dyspepsia cause or response.

added_items.final_wording: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

added_items.rationale: The exact alarm and endoscopy section supports clinician-assessed bleeding, medicine cause, new alarm features, available examination, and prior endoscopy context without asserting absence or ordering a procedure.

added_items.final_wording: Supports clinician-reviewed H. pylori testing context and annual review for people needing long-term dyspepsia management; medicine names and doses are not mapped.
